## Graph build/rebuild functions (Rust workspace)

Index-confirmed via `find_references` (mneme structural lookups):

| Symbol | Path | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `DbBuilder` (struct) | `store/src/builder.rs:23` | Primary builder type that owns the code-graph store construction. |
| `build_or_migrate` | `store/src/builder.rs:24`, `:46` | Entry point that builds a fresh graph DB or migrates an existing one. |
| `rebuild` | `store/src/builder.rs:30`, `:87` | Rebuilds the graph store from scratch (two definitions in the same file). |
| `build_graph` | `brain/src/cluster_runner.rs:143` | Constructs the graph used by the brain's cluster/community runner. |
| `parse_file` | `parsers/src/incremental.rs:103` | Parser entry that turns a single file into nodes/edges fed into the graph. |

Additional symbols listed as graph-build APIs in the project's own ground-truth doc (`docs/benchmarks/mcp-bench-2026-05-02/ground-truth.md`, retrieved via `mneme_context`) but **not resolvable via `find_references`** in the current index â€” treat as documentation references rather than confirmed graph hits:

- `cli/src/commands/build.rs` â€” `run`, `build_pipeline`, `index_files`
- `store/src/builder.rs` â€” `build_full`, `build_incremental`, `DbBuilder::new`
- `store/src/inject.rs` â€” `inject_file`, `inject_node`, `inject_edge`
- `store/src/lib.rs` â€” `Store::new`, `Store::open`
- `parsers/src/lib.rs` â€” `tree_to_nodes`
- `parsers/src/incremental.rs` â€” `IncrementalParser::new`, `parse_incremental`
- `brain/src/lib.rs` â€” graph orchestration entry

### Files actually used (citations)
- `brain/src/cluster_runner.rs` â€” `find_references(build_graph)`
- `store/src/builder.rs` â€” `find_references(rebuild | build_or_migrate | DbBuilder)`
- `parsers/src/incremental.rs` â€” `find_references(parse_file)`
- `docs/benchmarks/mcp-bench-2026-05-02/ground-truth.md` â€” `mneme_context(...)` retrieval

### Limitation
`find_references` returned empty for several names listed in the ground-truth doc (`build_full`, `build_incremental`, `inject_file/node/edge`, `parse_incremental`, `tree_to_nodes`, `build_pipeline`, `index_files`). Mneme's structural index didn't resolve them â€” they may exist under qualified names (e.g. `Store::open`, `IncrementalParser::parse_incremental`) that the current `find_references` symbol resolver doesn't match by short name. The MCP layer alone cannot fully confirm those without re-indexing or a qualified-name lookup.
